name: INDUSTRY Ontology (Porter's 5F, SWOT/TOWS, Ansoff Growth)
description: Industry-level strategic analysis: Porter's Five Forces, SWOT/TOWS synthesis, Ansoff Growth Matrix, and Competitive Positioning. References existing orgctx:CompetitiveForce and orgctx:Trend entities — adds analytical framework layers without duplication. TOWS generates candidate strategies flowing into VSOM. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SA sub-series.

components.PortersFiveForcesAnalysis: Container for five competitive forces assessment
components.ForceAssessment: Individual force rating with evidence and strategic implications
components.SWOTAnalysis: Four-quadrant strategic analysis linked to org context
components.SWOTFactor: Individual strength, weakness, opportunity, or threat with scoring
components.TOWSSynthesis: TOWS matrix generating candidate strategies from SWOT factor pairs
components.TOWSStrategy: Individual strategy from TOWS pairing with adoption tracking
components.AnsoffGrowthMatrix: Growth strategy analysis across market/product dimensions
components.AnsoffQuadrant: Individual quadrant with strategic options and risk profile

crossOntology.name: assessesForce
crossOntology.purpose: References existing competitive forces from ORG-CONTEXT

crossOntology.name: swotAddressesGap
crossOntology.purpose: SWOT weaknesses/threats map to gap analysis

versionHistory.changes: Initial release — Porter's 5F, SWOT/TOWS, Ansoff Growth, Competitive Positioning

notes: Layer 2 (Industry/Sector) of VSOM Strategy Analysis. References orgctx entities — no duplication. TOWS generates candidate strategies with adoption tracking flowing into vsom:StrategyComponent.
